title: Sort 'todo list' by priority then due date
author: dave
body: Closes #4. Sorts items high → normal → low, then by due date ascending within each bucket.
diff:
@@ -32,6 +32,15 @@
 def list_(overdue: bool) -> None:
     items = load_all()
     if overdue:
         items = core.filter_overdue(items)
+    PRIORITY_ORDER = {"high": 0, "normal": 1, "low": 2}
+    def sort_key(item):
+        return (
+            PRIORITY_ORDER.get(item["priority"], 1),
+            item.get("due") or "9999-99-99",
+        )
+    items = sorted(items, key=sort_key)
     for item in items:
         mark = "x" if item["done"] else " "
review_targets: completed items still appear in the list — should they be hidden, or shown but separated?
the 9999-99-99 sentinel is fragile — what if PyYAML serializes the date differently?
